# Cache Subsystem

The `engine/cache/` package is the Redis hot-path cache for a small allowlist of frequently-read runtime tables (kill switch, execution mode/health, broker order state, position baselines, strategy allocations, and feature snapshots). It uses write-through semantics behind the Postgres-backed runtime storage facade and a circuit breaker that falls through to Postgres loaders when Redis is unavailable. A bounded, one-second process-local L1 cache sits in front of the hottest typed wrappers after decode; it is an accelerator only, and Postgres remains the source of truth. Consumers are the typed wrappers in `wrappers/`, which the execution, risk, and oversight subsystems call instead of touching Redis directly.

## Files

- [redis_pool.py](redis_pool.py)
  Process-wide singleton `redis.Redis` client factory. Resolves the URL from `TS_REDIS_URL`/`REDIS_URL` (injecting a secret password when configured), sizes the pool via `TS_REDIS_POOL_SIZE`, and routes bounded health-check `PING`s through the circuit so a ping or missing-dependency failure opens the circuit instead of aborting the process. The pool pings on first use for a URL/pool-size key and then no more often than `TS_REDIS_POOL_HEALTHCHECK_INTERVAL_S` (default 30s), so hot cache operations do not pay a ping on every `redis_pool()` call while stale connections are still re-probed.
- [store.py](store.py)
  Write-through cache API: `read` (Redis-first, loader-populated on miss), `read_many` (MGET, one batch loader call for misses, pipelined Redis backfill), `write_through`/`write_through_many` (Postgres commit first, then Redis update after commit), `prime`, and `invalidate`. `write_through` performs one Redis `SET` after commit; it does not issue pre/post confirmatory `GET`s. `write_through_many` pipelines non-null cache updates into one Redis batch when the client supports pipelines and falls back to sequential `SET`s only for clients without a pipeline API. `read_many` returns one entry per distinct input key in first-seen order; duplicate inputs share one cache lookup, one loader result, and one backfill. Single-key and batch miss paths use process-local per-key single-flight locks with a Redis recheck after lock acquisition. A successful loader result, missing result, or loader exception is shared with concurrent waiters so a Redis miss burst does not turn into repeated database loader calls. Loader-populated Redis writes apply deterministic per-key TTL jitter so hot keys do not expire in one synchronized wave. All Redis calls go through `cache_circuit().call(...)`; failures degrade to Postgres rather than raising.
- [codec.py](codec.py)
  Canonical payload serialization. Wraps data in a versioned envelope (`{v, ts, data}`, `CURRENT_VERSION = 1`) using msgpack and rejects unexpected versions via `UnsupportedCacheVersion` so stale-schema payloads are detected and reloaded. Production-like runtimes, explicit `CACHE_CODEC_REQUIRE_MSGPACK=1`, and Redis live-cache mode require msgpack; if it is unavailable, encode/decode raise and startup/preflight readiness fails. JSON fallback is development-only and must be explicitly enabled with `CACHE_CODEC_ALLOW_JSON_FALLBACK=1`.
- [keys.py](keys.py)
  Centralized Redis keyspace builder. Enforces an allowlist of seven hot-path tables (`HOT_PATH_TABLES`) and a configurable prefix (`TS_REDIS_KEY_PREFIX`, default `trading`); unknown tables raise rather than being cached.
- [circuit.py](circuit.py)
  Consecutive-failure `CircuitBreaker` with cooldown probing and the `cache_circuit()` singleton. Defaults are `failure_threshold=3` and `cooldown_s=3.0` (`TS_REDIS_CIRCUIT_FAILURES`/`TS_REDIS_CIRCUIT_COOLDOWN_S`); opening or recovering emits `CACHE_REDIS_UNAVAILABLE`/`CACHE_REDIS_RECOVERED` alerts.
- [wrappers/_common.py](wrappers/_common.py)
  Shared helpers for the typed wrappers: JSON parse/dump, `now_ms`, one-second bounded L1 cache helpers, an explicit `L1_MISSING` negative-cache sentinel for safe absence caching, `after_commit_or_now` (defers cache priming until the supplied connection commits), and `reload_after_codec_version_mismatch` (invalidate, reload from the loader, and re-prime on a version mismatch).
- [wrappers/kill_switch.py](wrappers/kill_switch.py)
  Typed wrapper for `kill_switch_state` (short TTL, default 30s via `KILL_SWITCH_CACHE_TTL_S`). Fail-closed: an unreadable provider or stale/expired snapshot returns a global `provider_unavailable` block, and every write appends a hash-chained `kill_switch_audit` row. Its L1 cache only stores blocking snapshots in live-possible contexts; clear/non-blocking snapshots bypass L1 unless the process is explicitly in a non-live mode.
- [wrappers/execution_mode.py](wrappers/execution_mode.py)
  Typed wrapper for the singleton `execution_mode` row (`paper`/`shadow`/`live`, plus an `armed` flag). Arming `live` triggers `assert_live_execution_arming_preflight`, and each change appends a hash-chained `execution_mode_audit` row. L1 caches only states other than `live` + `armed=1`, so disabling live execution is never delayed by a permissive in-process snapshot.
- [wrappers/execution_health.py](wrappers/execution_health.py)
  Typed wrapper for the latest `execution_health_state` row (slippage/latency/routing/broker-failure metrics, 30s TTL).
- [wrappers/broker_order_state.py](wrappers/broker_order_state.py)
  Typed wrapper for `broker_order_state`, addressable by row id, source-order-id+symbol, or latest-by-symbol; a single write primes all applicable key forms via `write_through_many`.
- [wrappers/position_baseline.py](wrappers/position_baseline.py)
  Typed wrapper for per-broker `position_reconcile_baseline` snapshots used by reconciliation.
- [wrappers/strategy_allocations.py](wrappers/strategy_allocations.py)
  Typed wrapper for the latest `strategy_allocations` row per `window_days`.
- [wrappers/feature_snapshots.py](wrappers/feature_snapshots.py)
  Typed wrapper for the latest `model_feature_snapshots` row per symbol and feature-set tag. `latest_many(symbols, feature_group)` uses `store.read_many(...)` so multi-symbol prediction paths collapse Redis reads into one MGET and miss recovery into one latest-row database load plus one pipelined backfill. Decoded snapshots use the short L1 cache to avoid repeated Redis decode on hot prediction paths, and confirmed empty database results store a one-second L1 negative sentinel so repeated absent feature snapshots do not hammer Redis/Postgres. Provider/query failures are not negative-cached.

## Safety Boundary

Postgres is the source of truth; Redis is a disposable accelerator. The two invariants are enforced consistently across `store.py` and the wrappers:

- **Write-through ordering.** `write_through`/`write_through_many` open a `storage.transaction()` and persist to Postgres *first*; only after the transaction commits is Redis updated. A failure building or setting the cache value never rolls back the committed Postgres write — it logs and `invalidate`s the key so the next read repopulates from the loader. When a wrapper already holds a connection, `after_commit_or_now` defers the Redis prime until that connection commits.
- **No write-path readback.** Generic write-through cache updates do not read Redis to confirm a just-written value or to sample before/after codec versions. Correctness comes from Postgres-first ordering plus invalidation on write failure, while observability comes from `cache_write_through_path_total` tagged by `mode`, `result`, `key_count`, and payload codec version. Live price/feature snapshots are the path that needs stale-timestamp protection; that protection lives in the Redis Lua script described below.
- **Circuit-breaker fallback to Postgres.** Every Redis operation is wrapped by `cache_circuit().call(...)`. After consecutive failures (default 3) the circuit opens; reads then skip Redis and fall through to the database loader, and the cache transparently re-probes after the cooldown. A missing `redis` dependency or a failed startup ping force-opens the circuit so the process keeps running in Postgres fall-through mode.
- **Miss stampede control.** On a Redis miss, `read` acquires a per-key load lock, rechecks Redis, and only then calls the loader. `read_many` performs the same recheck for all distinct missed keys under sorted per-key locks, calls the supplied batch loader once for keys that are still missing, and backfills Redis through one pipeline. Duplicate input keys are de-duped before Redis and loader work while preserving first-seen output order. Concurrent waiters reuse the winner's in-process result when Redis backfill fails, and loader exceptions are propagated to waiters without retrying the same failing loader in every waiting thread. Lock waits are bounded by `TS_REDIS_SINGLEFLIGHT_LOCK_TIMEOUT_S` (default 5s); timeout fallbacks recheck Redis and then use the loader directly rather than blocking indefinitely. Released lock entries are removed immediately, with `TS_REDIS_SINGLEFLIGHT_MAX_LOCKS` as a cleanup guard for idle entries. Loader-populated backfills use deterministic TTL jitter derived from the cache key.
- **L1 safety policy.** The in-process L1 cache is deliberately shorter than Redis TTLs (`L1_HOT_WRAPPER_TTL_S = 1.0`) and bounded (`L1_HOT_WRAPPER_MAX_ENTRIES = 2048`). Wrapper writes explicitly invalidate the affected L1 key before replacing or re-priming it. Feature snapshots are the only current typed wrapper that stores L1 negatives, using `L1_NEGATIVE_CACHE_TTL_S = 1.0` only after a successful latest-row query proves the row is absent; writes replace the negative sentinel with the committed payload. Kill-switch L1 never serves a stale clear snapshot in live-possible contexts, execution-mode L1 never stores `live` + `armed=1`, and neither safety-critical live trading gate uses negative L1 entries; those rules keep L1 from making live execution more permissive than Redis/Postgres state.

On a codec version mismatch or decode failure, wrappers invalidate the key and reload from the loader rather than serving stale or unparseable bytes; the kill-switch wrapper additionally fails closed (global block) when the provider cannot be read.

Single-flight emits `cache_singleflight_waits_total`, `cache_singleflight_wins_total`, and `cache_singleflight_failures_total` through `engine.runtime.metrics`, tagged by `path` (`read` or `read_many`) and, for failures, `reason`. Write-through updates emit `cache_write_through_path_total`, tagged with the write mode (`single_set`, `pipeline_set_many`, `sequential_set_many`, or invalidation modes), result, key count, and payload codec version.

The runtime live-cache Redis backend also uses this envelope, but it is not a Postgres write-through cache. It stores live price/feature snapshots as binary payloads with `decode_responses=False` and updates each key through an atomic Lua `EVALSHA` script. The script reads the stored envelope timestamp and overwrites only when the incoming `snapshot_ts_ms` is not older, so stale live snapshots cannot clobber fresher ones. Full price/feature namespace clears scan only the configured live-cache prefix and delete matches in 512-key pipeline batches, preferring `UNLINK` and falling back to pipelined `DEL` for older clients/servers. `get_live_cache_snapshot()` exposes the active write path, script loads, evalsha attempts/results, NOSCRIPT reloads, stale rejections, write failures, accepted price/feature writes, bounded Redis health-check timing, and fallback reason/count; `live_cache_redis_write_path_total` emits the same Lua mode tagged by accepted, stale-rejected, or failed result.

## Configuration Families

- `TS_REDIS_*` — URL, pool size, socket timeouts, key prefix, bounded pool health-check interval, and circuit thresholds.
- `TS_REDIS_SINGLEFLIGHT_LOCK_TIMEOUT_S` / `TS_REDIS_SINGLEFLIGHT_MAX_LOCKS` — per-key miss lock wait bound and idle lock cleanup guard.
- `REDIS_URL` / `REDIS_PASSWORD_SECRET` — fallback URL and secret password source.
- `CACHE_CODEC_ALLOW_JSON_FALLBACK` — explicit non-production escape hatch for JSON envelope encoding when msgpack is unavailable. Keep `0` in production and compose templates.
- `CACHE_CODEC_REQUIRE_MSGPACK` — force the production codec requirement in local validation or dedicated smoke checks.
- `KILL_SWITCH_CACHE_TTL_S` — kill-switch snapshot freshness budget (clamped to `KILL_SWITCH_MAX_TTL_S`).
